name: Montejo de la Sierra, Manjirón, Puebla de la Sierra – circular route
name_original: Montejo de la Sierra, Manjirón, Puebla de la Sierra – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Horcajuelo De La Sierra navigate a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes, dense forests, and varied topography within the Sierra del Rincón Biosphere Reserve. The region features numerous mountain passes and quiet, well-paved roads, offering a demanding yet rewarding experience for cyclists. Expect long climbs with substantial elevation gains, often exceeding 1000 meters, through green valleys and natural scenery. The area's roads are generally narrow and experience low traffic, providing an immersive cycling environment. Best road cycling routes around Horcajuelo De La Sierra The most popular road cycling route is Montejo de la Sierra, Manjirón, Puebla de la Sierra – circular route, a 51.7 miles (83.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 40 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 1600 meters of elevation gain through varied mountain terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Serna del Monte, El Cardoso de la Sierra – circular route, a difficult 38.2 miles (61.5 km) path. This route includes over 1200 meters of climbing, traversing mountain passes and quiet roads. Local road cyclists also love the El Villar Reservoir and Gorge – Ascent to Puerto de La Puebla loop from Prádena del Rincón, a 50.1 miles (80.6 km) trail leading through mountain passes and reservoir views, often completed in about 4 hours 13 minutes. Road cycling around Horcajuelo De La Sierra is defined by challenging mountain passes, quiet roads, and diverse forests, with routes offering significant elevation changes. The network includes options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Horcajuelo De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 27 reviews. More than 4000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Horcajuelo De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Horcajuelo De La Sierra?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Horcajuelo De La Sierra, offering a range of experiences. You'll find 8 moderate routes and 12 difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Horcajuelo De La Sierra?
A: Road cycling in Horcajuelo De La Sierra is characterized by significant elevation changes and challenging mountain passes. The region features long, constant climbs with gradients often exceeding +1000 to +2000 meters. Roads are generally well-paved, narrow, and experience little traffic, providing a tranquil experience amidst varied topography, dense forests, and impressive rock formations.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are circular. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977461099/montejo-de-la-sierra-manjiron-puebla-de-la-sierra-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montejo de la Sierra, Manjirón, Puebla de la Sierra – circular route&lt;/a&gt; covers 83.3 km with over 1600 meters of elevation gain. Another option is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977469461/la-serna-del-monte-el-cardoso-de-la-sierra-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serna del Monte, El Cardoso de la Sierra – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, a 61.5 km path with over 1200 meters of climbing.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: Horcajuelo De La Sierra is known for its challenging terrain. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39837333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de La Puebla (1636 m) – La Hiruela loop from Prádena del Rincón&lt;/a&gt; offer significant climbs, with this specific route featuring over 1700 meters of elevation gain over 67.4 km. Many routes include multiple ascents of passes like Puerto del Cardoso, Puerto de La Hiruela, and Puerto de la Puebla.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: You'll encounter stunning natural beauty and cultural heritage. Highlights include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2322047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera de los Litueros Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, especially vibrant in spring. You can also cycle through the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birch Forest of Somosierra&lt;/a&gt;, or pass through traditional villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2794055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hiruela&lt;/a&gt;, known for its preserved architecture.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or offer water views?
A: Yes, some routes offer scenic views of reservoirs. The difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31528830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Reservoir and Gorge – Ascent to Puerto de La Puebla loop from Prádena del Rincón&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option, providing views of the reservoir and gorge while tackling mountain passes.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Horcajuelo De La Sierra?
A: The road cycling routes in Horcajuelo De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 27 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, quiet roads, and the stunning natural scenery within the Sierra del Rincón Biosphere Reserve.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Horcajuelo De La Sierra?
A: The region offers diverse landscapes that change with the seasons. While specific recommendations depend on personal preference for weather and foliage, spring and autumn are generally ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors. The Chorrera de los Litueros Waterfall is particularly spectacular during thaw and spring.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty road cycling routes for those seeking less intense climbs?
A: Yes, while many routes are difficult, there are moderate options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33722258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buitrago de Lozoya – El Villar Reservoir and Gorge loop from La Serna del Monte&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 51.7 km route with around 746 meters of elevation gain, offering a rewarding experience without the most extreme ascents.

Q: Can I find panoramic viewpoints on the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region's numerous mountain passes provide breathtaking panoramic views. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6527980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tres Provincias (Cebollera Vieja)&lt;/a&gt;, located at over 2100 meters, offers extensive views in all directions, making the challenging climbs well worth the effort.

Q: What makes Horcajuelo De La Sierra a unique road cycling destination?
A: Horcajuelo De La Sierra stands out due to its location within the Sierra del Rincón Biosphere Reserve, ensuring a backdrop of protected natural beauty. The combination of challenging, Vuelta a España-worthy mountain passes, quiet and well-paved roads, and diverse ecosystems—from dense forests to high peaks—creates an immersive and rewarding cycling experience.
tips: The Puentes Viejas reservoir was built on the Lozoya River and is part of the water supply network known as Canal de Isabel II, which is still in operation after several centuries in use. It is also a panoramic site with very nice views.
This dam offers spectacular views. It is worth going there. Ideal for hiking, motorbike or road bike excursions. Next to the dam there is a small parking lot for about 10 cars.
Spectacular section along the M130 between a beautiful gorge and ascent to the port of La Puebla. Be careful with the last kilometer of this.
La Puebla, a port with two slopes, has a rough start but before the second kilometer it softens.
The surroundings of this Madrid town are an extraordinary place to go on a bike route, especially during autumn, due to the abundance of deciduous trees.

waypoints.name: Ascent to Puerto de La Puebla
